{"id":"smallhold-dx55","title":"DELETE /api/v1/statuses/:id: verify ownership, delete, enqueue Delete activity","description":"Delete a post. Verify authed account owns the post (not just same instance — exact account_id match). Delete posts row. Clean up mention rows, hashtag rows, idempotency key if any. Enqueue Delete{Tombstone} activity. Trigger streaming delete event. Return the deleted Status object (required by Mastodon API — clients expect it).

Test discipline:
- Delete own post → returns deleted Status, post gone from DB
- Delete another account's post → 403
- Delete non-existent post → 404
- Delete boost → removes the boost post, sends Undo{Announce} not Delete{Note}
- Verify streaming 'delete' event emitted with correct status ID

Defensive programming:
- Transaction: delete post + clean up related rows atomically
- Tombstone: Delete activity must reference the ap_id (ActivityPub URI), not the numeric DB ID
- Media files associated with deleted post: set media.post_id = NULL (don't delete media — user may have saved it)","acceptance_criteria":"Own post deleted with 200; other's post returns 403; non-existent returns 404; Delete activity enqueued; streaming delete event emitted; media post_id unlinked","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:35:18Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:35:18Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-3qp","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:35:23Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
